channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - wccftech.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for wccftech.com

item.title: “Bootleg of a Bootleg”: A New Pokémon Lookalike, Pickmon, Seems to be Ripping Off Both Palworld and Pokémon
item.content:encoded:
When Palworld was first announced back in 2021, the internet immediately clocked it as 'Pokémon with guns,' and it was the least surprising thing for Nintendo to initiate a lawsuit against the game's developer, PocketPair, even if it meant trying some "Hail Mary" tactics by going after patents for game mechanics, rather than attempting to catch PocketPair on copyright infringement with the design of the Pals coming very close to the design of popular Pokémon.

But even amidst the lawsuit, Palworld has been a smash success - it may never arrive on a Nintendo console, but it doesn't have to when it's already a hugely popular game on PC, Xbox, and PlayStation. There's even a farming spin-off game already in the works, and Palworld is due to launch out of early access later this year.

Now, another game is trying to do what Palworld has done, which is launch a successful game off the back of looking almost exactly like Pokémon without being Pokémon, while practically goading Nintendo into launching a lawsuit against it. But this game may have a more difficult time.

Pickmon (the name doesn't do it any favours) is perhaps best described by X (formerly Twitter) user Noriofan, who called it a "bootleg of a bootleg" when they replied to Pickmon's debut trailer on X.

Developer PocketGame describes Pickmon as "a brand new multiplayer monster-collecting game with open-world survival elements," with its trailer showing clips of a player character and their Pikachu-lookalike pal adventuring with them as they build up a base with other 'Pickmon' (the name for the creatures featured in the trailer), flying across the vast land with a glider, and capturing presumably wild Pickmon with magic cards as Pickmon's brand of Poké balls.

Which, interestingly enough, suggests that PocketGame has been paying attention to Nintendo's lawsuit against PocketPair, opting not to go with an orb-shaped object and ensuring that players use gliders and not flying Pickmon to get around to avoid some of the patent issues that have already forced PocketPair to make changes to Palworld.

It'll be interesting to see if Pickmon is able to get away with its character and game design inspirations. We know that Nintendo is not afraid to sue whoever it wants to, and that we can probably expect a suit to be launched when Nintendo feels it is the right time.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/pokemon-palworld-rip-off-pickmon-announced/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Cyrondiil Doesn’t Belong To Lone Heroes Anymore – Oblivion Remastered Is Getting Modded Multiplayer
item.content:encoded:
"For 20 years, Cyrodiil belonged to heroes walking alone… Not anymore!" announced the reveal teaser trailer for an Oblivion Remastered multiplayer mod which promises to shake up the remake's experience considerably with community-driven features.

In development by ReadyM, the development team behind the WukongMP mod, which brought PvP and co-op to Black Myth Wukong, this mod will deliver community-driven multiplayer, allowing users to run massive community servers and "experience roleplaying scenarios, battle arenas, establish player guilds, and crawl dungeons with friends."

Although short, the teaser trailer shows just enough for fans of last year's remake to get excited about a new way to experience what is considered among the finest entries in the series created by Bethesda. To stay up to date, you can join the mod's official Discord server.

Due to a lack of modding support and obvious technical reasons, this upcoming Oblivion Remastered multiplayer mod won't be hitting consoles, where the game still managed to be successful enough. Player retention, on the other hand, hasn't been great, as around half of the game's players on PlayStation 5 explored the new rendition of Cyrodiil for less than 15 hours, which is a really small amount of time for a game as massive as this, showing how nostalgia can be powerful, but sometimes not enough to retain players for the entire duration of a game's main campaign.

Although PC players will be the only ones who will be able to explore and live in Cyrodiil together, more heroes are expected to arrive in the land soon. Oblivion Remastered is coming to Nintendo Switch 2 later this year, following Fallout 4: Anniversary Edition, which launched last month, and Indiana Jones and the Great Circle, which launches on May 12.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/oblivion-remastered-multiplayer/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Nacon-Published The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu Will Arrive on PC, Xbox Series, and PS5 in July 2026
item.content:encoded:
The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu, one of the Lovecraftian games set to arrive this year from French publisher Nacon, the same publisher that is on the brink of liquidation, has an official release date. After Nacon and developer ACE Team revealed it would arrive sometime in Summer 2026 back in November 2025, we now know it'll arrive on PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X/S on July 15, 2026.

Last week, Nacon was supposed to host its annual Nacon Connect showcase, which would've likely included today's release date announcement for The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu, along with looks at other games Nacon has coming for the rest of 2026, including Cthulhu: The Cosmic Abyss, which is set to arrive next month on April 16, 2026.

Days before the showcase was set to take place, Nacon filed for insolvency following an issue with its majority shareholder. While the company's fate is not yet sealed, liquidation is a very real possibility, putting the dozen studios Nacon owns and dozens of projects it has underway, between the ones we know about and the ones still unannounced, very much at risk.

That said, the company has been adamant that "this is not the end," and confirmed that it would see its current suite of announced titles through to their release, hence today's announcement and the above release date trailer, and confirmed the new window for the Nacon Connect showcase, which will now take place in May 2026.

The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu is a four-player co-op game where players are dropped on an island to explore a cursed jungle that can make you believe your own teammates have become enemies as your player characters slowly go mad on the island.

You'll have to survive the creatures that plague the island while convincing your teammates that you are still one of them. It's an interesting twist on what otherwise appears to be a classic case of co-op PvE gameplay packaged within an incredibly atmospheric Lovecraftian presentation.

Let's hope Nacon is still around to publish it by the time it arrives on July 15, 2026.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/the-mound-omen-of-cthulhu-release-date-july-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
